ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Juda da yɛl bama la ye, “Ti ya'a kʋʋ ti pitʋ nwa' su'a di nyɔɔd an bɔ tisi ti?
translated: And Judah said to his brothers, “What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?

ref: When Jacob saw her and his uncle's sheep, he rolled the rock away and watered the sheep.
text: Ka Jakob n nyɛ Rakɛl onɛ aan o ansib Laban pu'ayua la nɛ Laban kɔnbid la, o keŋ nɛ bilig kugir la yis bulig la nɔɔrin ka kɛ ka o ansib kɔnbid la nu.
translated: And Jacob loved Rachel, and he said, I will serve you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter, and Laban said to him, it is better for me to give her to you than to another man, so stay with me.

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Ka Esau lɛbisi yɛl o ye, “Ya'a aan ala, fʋn kɛl ka m basim dap sieba ka ba sʋŋif ka gurif.” Ka Jakob ye, “M bierɛ, m pʋ'ʋs ya, amaa li sia'al ya.”
translated: And Esau said to him, “Let me eat some of that red stuff, for I am famished.” And Jacob said, “First sell me your birthright.”

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Duom giligi kaae gɔs teŋ la wa'alim nɛ di labisim ka m ye zaŋ nɛ tisif!”
translated: Let us go and tell the chief that the children are suffering and it is up to us to help them!

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: ka yɛl Efiron ba nifin ye, “M ba', kɛlisima! M bɔɔd ye fʋ kuos paalʋ la tisim ka di'e li ligidi, ka m na nyaŋi mumi m kum anina.”
translated: And the people of Ephron answered him, "My lord, listen to me; I give you the field and the cave that is in it; in the presence of my people I give it to you; bury your dead."
